title: Trigonometry II: Identities, Compound Angles and Equations
description: Trigonometric identities, compound angle and double angle formulas, solving trigonometric equations over a given interval, and the R sin(x+alpha) harmonic form for a sinx + b cosx.
objectives: Use sin^2(theta) + cos^2(theta) = 1 and tan(theta) = sin(theta)/cos(theta) to simplify expressions and prove identities
Apply the compound angle formulas for sin(A±B), cos(A±B) and tan(A±B)
Apply the double angle formulas for sin(2A), cos(2A) and tan(2A), including the three forms of cos(2A)
Solve trigonometric equations over a given interval, finding all solutions using identities and the symmetries of the trig graphs
Express a sin(x) + b cos(x) in the harmonic form R sin(x + alpha) or R cos(x + alpha) and use this to solve equations and find maxima/minima

lessons.title: Trig identities and compound angle formulas
Solving trig equations and the harmonic form
